Vision

To cultivate a generation of conscious, socially responsible changemakers through the arts.

Mission

To harness the power of spoken word and multidisciplinary arts to inspire thought, spark dialogue, and drive social transformation.

Powering Creativity Since 2000

A Nairobi-based collective of writers, artists, and performers building the infrastructure for African creative expression.

Poetry Slam Africa · Cheche Sessions · Nuetry Africa · Spills House · The Bard Network

2026–27 Reigning Grand Slam Champion

Timelines

Grand Slam Champion · Kenya's Representative · 2026 World Slam Championships, Durban

The reigning Poetry Slam Africa Grand Slam Champion and Kenya's flag-bearer at the 2026 World Poetry Slam Championships in Durban, South Africa. A voice that has defined the current era of Kenyan spoken word — precise, political, and profoundly human.

View Slam Royalty →

Season Update · Poetry Slam Africa

The 2026–27 Season Has Moved to Braeburn — Here's What That Means

For the first time in Poetry Slam Africa's history, the competition opens its season at Braeburn Theatre. We unpack the move, the ambition behind it, and what it demands of the community that shows up for it.

Since 2008 · Nairobi, Kenya

Poetry Slam Africa

Kenya's premier competitive spoken word platform, running since 2008. Three quarterly preliminary events serve as the pathway to the season-ending Grand Slam Finals. The 2026–27 season runs at Braeburn Theatre, Gitanga Road, Nairobi.

14 Jun 2026

1st Preliminary

Braeburn Theatre, Gitanga Road · 2:00 PM – 6:00 PM

Completed
Sep 2026

2nd Preliminary

Braeburn Theatre, Gitanga Road · TBC

Upcoming
Nov 2026

3rd Preliminary

Braeburn Theatre, Gitanga Road · TBC

Upcoming
28–31 Jan 2027

Grand Slam Finals — Poetry Slam Africa Festival

Venue TBC · The crowning event of the 2026–27 season

Grand Finale

Schools Programme · Since 2016

Rising Voices Schools' Slam

A youth-centred spoken word development programme nurturing creativity, confidence, and critical thinking among students through school-based workshops, mentorship, and competitive slam platforms.

Programme

School Workshops

Facilitator-led sessions bringing slam poetry into the classroom — writing, performance, and civic voice.

Competition

Inter-School Slam

Schools compete in qualifying rounds culminating in a national schools' slam final.

Partnership

Still I Rise

In collaboration with Still I Rise International School — curriculum integration and facilitator capacity.

Hall of Champions

Slam Royalty

The poets who have stood on the Poetry Slam Africa stage and taken the crown. A living archive of Kenya's most powerful spoken word voices.

2026–27 · Reigning Champion

Timelines

Grand Slam Champion · Kenya at World Slams 2026 (Durban)

The reigning Grand Slam Champion and Kenya's representative at the 2026 World Poetry Slam Championships in Durban, South Africa.

2025

Dominion

Runner-Up · World Poetry Slam Championships 2025

A PSA alumnus who rose to claim second place at the 2025 World Poetry Slam Championships — the highest finish for a Kenyan poet in the competition's history.

2022

Slim Shaka

Finalist · World Poetry Slam Championships 2022

A groundbreaking Sheng wordsmith whose work dismantles the boundaries between spoken word and hip-hop.

Alumni

Mufasa Poet

Actor · Teaching Artist · Kenya's Most Prominent Poet

A transformative figure in East African spoken word — actor, teaching artist, and widely regarded as Kenya's most prominent poet.

Alumni

Gufy

Spotify Spoken Word Ambassador · Filmmaker

Named Spotify's spoken word ambassador, Gufy has bridged the gap between poetry performance and digital distribution across the continent.

Alumni

Hart the Band

Kenya's Foremost Poetry Ensemble

Born from the PSA stage, Hart the Band expanded spoken word into full band performance — Kenya's foremost poetry ensemble.

Digital & Live · Since 2019

Poets You Should Know

A showcase platform for exceptional African poets — through performances, interviews, and publications. Monthly open mics, special editions, and the annual Women's Slam close the year.

Monthly

PYSK Open Mic

Monthly · All voices welcome · Venue rotates

Recurring
Annual

PYSK Francophone Slam

A special edition celebrating poetry in French from across the continent

Special Edition
Annual · June

PYSK Katiba Day

Spoken word in conversation with Kenya's constitution — civic poetry at its most direct

Special Edition
Annual · December

PYSK Women's Slam

The final and flagship PYSK event of the year — celebrating women's voices in spoken word

Flagship

Audio Documentation

Sauti Dokezi

Sauti Dokezi — "the recorded voice" — is Creative Spills' audio documentation and podcast project. Capturing spoken word performances, artist interviews, and cultural conversations from across East Africa.

🎙

Episode 12 — The Grand Slam Files

42 min · June 2026

🎙

Episode 11 — Women in Slam

38 min · May 2026

🎙

Episode 10 — The Bard Network

51 min · Apr 2026

Pan-African · Since 2025

Nuetry Africa

A pan-African poetry platform dedicated to nuanced poetry and new voices shaping the continent's literary future. Operating through a growing network of franchise chapters delivering open mics, workshops, publications, and digital storytelling.

Publication

The Nuetry Journal

Original poetry and prose from African writers — digital and print editions quarterly.

Workshop

Writing Labs

Facilitated creative writing workshops open to emerging voices across the continent.

Network

Franchise Chapters

A growing continental network of Nuetry chapters running open mics and showcases locally.

Balance · Form · Craft

Mizani

Mizani — Swahili for "scales" or "balance" — is Creative Spills' craft-focused programme examining the formal elements of spoken word: metre, breath, structure, and performance.

Craft Sessions

Intensive craft-focused workshops led by senior practitioners — from breath work to structural form.

Mizani Press

Short-form craft essays and performance notes distributed online and to PSA participants.

Since 2000 · East Africa & Diaspora

The Bard Network

Established in 2000, The Bard Network is a collaborative ecosystem connecting poets, storytellers, educators, and cultural institutions across Africa and the diaspora — addressing the fragmentation of the poetry ecosystem.

Directory

Artist Network

A living directory of spoken word artists across Kenya, Uganda, Tanzania, Rwanda, and the diaspora.

Mobility

Touring & Exchange

Facilitating cross-border touring, residencies, and collaborative performance opportunities.

Advocacy

Policy & Sector

Collective advocacy for recognition, resources, and rights for spoken word practitioners in the region.

Creative Spills Sound

Music Projects

Since 2021 · Monthly · Nairobi

Cheche Sessions

A curated interdisciplinary performance and dialogue platform featuring spoken word, music, visual arts, and film. Monthly gathering at Spills House around socially relevant themes.

View upcoming sessions →

Label · Collective

Miale

Creative Spills' music arm — a label and collective recording, releasing, and distributing original East African sound. Miale artists draw from the full spectrum of the region's musical heritage and contemporary influence.

Listen to releases →

Tour · On the Road

Cheche on the Road

The roving edition of Cheche Sessions — taking the sound to cities, festivals, and stages across Kenya and the continent. Next stop: Mombasa, August 2026.

See tour dates →

Upcoming · Vol. 12

Cheche Sessions Vol. 12

The 12th edition of our flagship monthly session. Lineup and date to be announced. Tickets available in advance through the Creative Spills platform.

Get Tickets

Creative Residency · Nairobi

Spills
House.

Our creative residency space — a home for writers, musicians, and cultural practitioners to work, rest, and build. Structured residency programmes alongside an open community workspace.

Applications accepted on a rolling basis. Residencies run from two weeks to three months. Space is limited and competitive.

Studio Space

Recording and rehearsal facilities

Writers' Room

Quiet workspace with reference library

Community Kitchen

Shared dining for residents

Event Space

Performance space for up to 60

Mentorship

Access to Creative Spills network

Documentation

Support via Frontrow Africa

Apply for Residency

Applications Open — Q3 Cohort

Rolling deadline · 2–12 week residencies · Nairobi

Q4 Cohort — Accepting Expressions of Interest

Priority given to music and interdisciplinary practitioners

The Creative Spills Newsroom

From the Collective

Featured · Season Update

The 2026–27 Season Has Moved Stages — Here's What That Means

For the first time in Poetry Slam Africa's history, the competition leaves the Goethe-Institut and opens its season at Braeburn Theatre. We unpack what the move signals about where the platform is headed — and what it demands of the community that shows up for it.

By Creative Spills Editorial · June 2026 · 8 min read
